response:
# Combobox with Autocomplete Accessibility Design Plan > **For Claude:** Use a11y-planner protocol. Review with a11y-critic after implementation. > **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA > **Users who need accessibility:** Screen reader users, keyboard-only users, low vision users, mobile/touch users, users with motor disabilities > **Assistive technologies:** NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver macOS, keyboard-only navigation **Feature:** Reusable React combobox search input with async autocomplete results, grouped options, keyboard and mouse selection, clear control, and dynamic result updates. **Risk Level:** Medium **Component/Page Type:** Editable Combobox with List Autocomplete --- ## Scope & Context This component lets users type into a search field, receive filtered autocomplete suggestions, navigate suggestions with the keyboard, select a result, clear the value, and reopen the dropdown. Results may be fetched asynchronously from an API and may be grouped under headings such as “People” or “Organizations.” The component must follow the WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide Combobox pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/combobox/ Primary accessibility risks: - Keyboard users must be able to type, open, navigate, select, close, clear, and exit without losing context. - Screen reader users must hear the input name, combobox role, expanded/collapsed state, active option, result count, loading state, empty state, and selected value. - Focus must remain on the input while navigating options using `aria-activedescendant`; DOM focus should not move into the popup list. - Async loading must not create silent UI changes. - Grouped results must be perceivable without making option navigation noisy or ambiguous. - Mobile/touch users must have usable target sizes and clear interaction affordances. This plan is not claiming that every screen reader/browser pairing will announce the pattern identically. It is claiming the implementation will expose the correct accessible name, role, value, state, relationships, focus model, and status messages required for WCAG 2.2 AA and APG conformance. --- ## Semantic Structure Plan The combobox should be implemented as a labeled form control within an optional search form or field group. Use native input semantics wherever possible and add ARIA only for the composite combobox behavior. ### Structure Rules - The visible label must be associated with the input using `label for`. - WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - WCAG 2.4.6 Headings and Labels - The input is the active combobox control and keeps DOM focus. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - The popup uses `role="listbox"`. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - Each selectable result uses `role="option"`. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - Grouped results may use `role="group"` with `aria-labelledby` pointing to a visible group heading. - WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - A status region outside the listbox announces loading, result count, no results, and selection. - WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages - Do not use `title` as the accessible name. Use visible label text or `aria-label` only where a visible label is not possible. - WCAG 2.4.6 Headings and Labels - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value ### Structure Diagram ```html

Type to search. Use arrow keys to review suggestions.

People
Jane Smith
Organizations
Zivtech
``` Note: This is a structure stub, not production code. --- ## Interaction Pattern Design ### APG Mapping Primary pattern: **WAI-ARIA APG Combobox Pattern** https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/combobox/ Specific variant: **Editable combobox with list autocomplete, manual selection.** The input value updates only when the user explicitly selects an option with Enter, click/tap, or equivalent pointer activation. Arrowing through options updates `aria-activedescendant` and visual active styling, but should not overwrite the input value unless the product intentionally chooses automatic selection. For this fixture, use manual selection because it is safer for search/filter contexts. ### Interactive Elements Table | Widget | APG Pattern | Keyboard Model | ARIA Attributes | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Search input combobox | APG Combobox: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/combobox/ | Typing filters; Down opens/moves to first option; Up opens/moves to last option; Enter selects active option; Escape closes; Tab exits | `role="combobox"`, `aria-autocomplete="list"`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, `aria-activedescendant`, `aria-describedby` | 2.1.1, 2.4.3, 3.3.2, 4.1.2 | | Suggestions popup | APG Listbox popup within Combobox | Not directly tabbable; controlled from input with arrow keys | `role="listbox"`, accessible name via `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`, `hidden` when closed | 1.3.1, 4.1.2 | | Result option | APG Option inside Listbox | Active via Arrow Up/Down; selected via Enter or click/tap | `role="option"`, stable `id`, `aria-selected` on active/selected option | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 | | Result group | APG Listbox grouped options | Arrow navigation skips group heading and moves only through options | `role="group"`, `aria-labelledby` referencing visible group label | 1.3.1, 4.1.2 | | Clear button | APG Button: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/button/ | Tab focuses; Enter/Space clears input and results | Native button, `aria-label="Clear search"` if icon-only | 2.1.1, 2.5.8, 4.1.2 | | Optional open/reopen control | APG Button, if separate control exists | Tab focuses; Enter/Space opens popup | Native button, `aria-label="Show suggestions"`, `aria-expanded` if it controls popup | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 | ### Keyboard Model Input focused, popup closed: - Typing: updates input value and starts filtering or async fetch. - Arrow Down: opens popup if results exist, sets active descendant to first option. - Arrow Up: opens popup if results exist, sets active descendant to last option. - Enter: submits search only when no option is active; selects active option when one is active. - Escape: clears active descendant and closes popup. If popup is already closed, optional behavior may clear the input only if product explicitly wants that behavior. - Tab: moves focus to the next focusable element and closes popup without changing the input value. Input focused, popup open: - Arrow Down: moves active descendant to next enabled option. Wrap only if the product wants cyclic navigation; default recommendation is no wrap to reduce surprise. - Arrow Up: moves active descendant to previous enabled option. - Home/End: preserve native text editing behavior when cursor is in input. Do not hijack Home/End for list navigation unless using a documented variant and testing confirms screen reader compatibility. - Enter: selects active option, updates input value, closes popup, announces selection. - Escape: closes popup, clears `aria-activedescendant`, keeps focus in input. - Tab: closes popup and moves focus onward. Do not trap focus. - Printable characters: update input text, reset active descendant, fetch/filter results. Pointer and touch: - Clicking or tapping an option selects it, updates input value, closes popup, and returns focus to the input. - Touch targets must be at least 24x24 CSS px, with 44x44 recommended. - WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size - Mouse hover may visually highlight an option, but must not be the only way to set the active option. Keyboard state remains authoritative for keyboard users. - WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard --- ## ARIA Implementation Plan ### Input Required: - `role="combobox"` - Exposes the input as a composite autocomplete control. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - `aria-autocomplete="list"` - Communicates that suggested values appear in a list. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - `aria-expanded="false"` when popup is closed and `"true"` when open. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - `aria-controls="search-combobox-listbox"` - Links the input to the controlled popup. - WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships; 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - `aria-activedescendant="option-id"` when an option is active. - Keeps DOM focus on input while exposing the active option. - WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order; 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - `aria-describedby="search-combobox-help search-combobox-status"` - Associates instructions and dynamic status with the control. - WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships; 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions Conditionally required: - `aria-invalid="true"` only if the combobox is part of a required/validated form and the current value is invalid. - WCAG 3.3.1 Error Identification; 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - `aria-required="true"` only if using ARIA-required semantics; prefer native `required` when valid for the form behavior. - WCAG 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions; 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value Use `aria-controls` rather than relying only on `aria-owns`. `aria-owns` may be considered only if the popup must be rendered outside the DOM subtree through a portal and testing shows the relationship needs reinforcement. Do not use both casually; duplicated relationship semantics can become noisy. ### Listbox Required when rendered: - `role="listbox"` - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - Accessible name through `aria-label="Search suggestions"` or `aria-labelledby`. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - `hidden`, conditional rendering, or equivalent when closed. - Hidden content must not remain keyboard or screen-reader reachable. - WCAG 1.3.2 Meaningful Sequence; 2.4.3 Focus Order Async loading: - Use `aria-busy="true"` on the listbox container or combobox region while results are updating. - WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages - Set `aria-busy="false"` after the result set is stable. - WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages ### Options Each selectable result: - `role="option"` - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - Stable unique `id` - Needed by `aria-activedescendant`. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - `aria-selected="true"` for the currently active option in the list; `"false"` for other visible options. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value Disabled/unavailable options: - Avoid rendering unavailable items as options if they cannot be selected. - If unavoidable, use `aria-disabled="true"` and skip them in arrow navigation. - WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard; 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value ### Groups For grouped results: - Group container: `role="group"` - Group label: visible text with an `id` - Group container: `aria-labelledby="[group-label-id]"` Screen reader announcement target: “People, Jane Smith, option” or equivalent. The group heading should provide context without becoming an extra arrow-key stop. --- ## Focus Management Plan Focus must stay on the input during typing and option navigation. This is the central combobox design decision. ### Focus Behavior 1. User Tabs into the input. - Screen reader announces label, role, current value, collapsed/expanded state, and instructions. - WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order; 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value 2. User types. - DOM focus remains on input. - Popup opens when results are available or loading begins. - `aria-expanded` changes to `"true"`. - `aria-busy` and live region communicate loading if async. - WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard; 4.1.3 Status Messages 3. User presses Arrow Down or Arrow Up. - DOM focus remains on input. - `aria-activedescendant` updates to the active option id. - Visual active styling moves to the matching option. - The active option is scrolled into view if needed without moving DOM focus. - WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order; 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value 4. User presses Enter with an active option. - Input value updates to the selected option label. - Popup closes. - `aria-expanded` becomes `"false"`. - `aria-activedescendant` is cleared. - Status region announces selection, e.g., “Jane Smith selected.” - Focus remains on input. - WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard; 4.1.3 Status Messages 5. User presses Escape. - Popup closes. - Active descendant clears. - Focus remains on input. - Input value remains unchanged unless product explicitly defines Escape-to-clear when already closed. - WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard; 2.4.3 Focus Order 6. User presses Tab. - Popup closes. - Focus moves to the next tabbable element. - No selection occurs unless an option was already selected through Enter or pointer activation. - WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard; 2.4.3 Focus Order ### Tab Order Recommended order: 1. Search input combobox 2. Clear button, only when visible and useful 3. Next page or form control The listbox options are not in the Tab order. They are reached through arrow-key navigation while focus remains on the input. ### Hidden Content Strategy When the popup is closed: - Prefer conditional rendering or the `hidden` attribute. - Do not leave visually hidden options exposed to assistive technology. - Do not rely on `aria-hidden="true"` alone if descendants can still receive focus. - WCAG 1.3.2 Meaningful Sequence; 2.4.3 Focus Order ### Focus Indicator - Input, clear button, and any optional open button must have visible `:focus-visible` styles. - Use a two-color focus indicator so the ring remains visible on varied backgrounds. - Focus indicator contrast must meet 3:1 against adjacent colors. - WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible - WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured - WCAG 2.4.13 Focus Appearance --- ## State Communication Design ### State Communication Table | State | Visual Indicator | Programmatic Indicator | ARIA Attribute | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Empty input | Placeholder or helper text, not label replacement | Visible label remains associated | `label for`, optional `aria-describedby` | 1.3.1, 2.4.6, 3.3.2 | | User typing | Text appears in input | Native input value | Native input value | 4.1.2 | | Popup closed | Dropdown not visible | Combobox collapsed | `aria-expanded="false"` | 4.1.2 | | Popup open | Dropdown visible below input | Combobox expanded and controls listbox | `aria-expanded="true"`, `aria-controls` | 1.3.1, 4.1.2 | | Loading | Spinner plus “Loading suggestions” text or status | Status announcement and busy state | `aria-busy="true"`, `role="status"` | 4.1.3 | | Loaded results | Result list visible, count may appear visually | Polite result count announcement | `role="status"`, `aria-live="polite"` | 4.1.3 | | No results | Visible “No results found” message | Polite no-results announcement | `role="status"` | 4.1.3 | | Active option | Highlighted row, not color alone | Active descendant points to option | `aria-activedescendant="[option-id]"`, `aria-selected="true"` | 1.4.1, 4.1.2 | | Option not active | Default row style | Not selected | `aria-selected="false"` | 4.1.2 | | Selected option | Input value updates, dropdown closes | Selection announced | Status message, input value | 4.1.2, 4.1.3 | | Disabled option | Muted style plus text/icon if shown | Disabled state | `aria-disabled="true"` | 1.4.1, 4.1.2 | | Invalid input | Error text, icon, border | Invalid state and associated error | `aria-invalid="true"`, `aria-describedby` | 1.3.1, 3.3.1, 4.1.2 | | Cleared input | Input becomes empty, clear button hidden | Value removed, status optional | Native input value; status message if needed | 4.1.2, 4.1.3 | ### Live Region Strategy Use one status region for combobox events. Do not create a live region per option or per group. Recommended polite announcements: - “Loading suggestions.” - “5 suggestions available.” - “No suggestions found.” - “Jane Smith selected.” - “Search cleared.” Use `role="status"` with `aria-live="polite"` and `aria-atomic="true"`. Avoid assertive announcements for normal filtering and navigation. Assertive output would interrupt typing and make the component harder to use. ### Result Count Announcements Announce result counts after async loading or filtering settles, not on every keystroke synchronously. Debounce announcements enough to avoid chatter while preserving timely feedback. Examples: - After results load: “7 suggestions available.” - After grouped results load: “7 suggestions available in 2 groups.” - Empty result: “No suggestions found.” ### Option Announcement Strategy Each option’s accessible name should include the primary result label and only essential disambiguating metadata. Good: - “Jane Smith, Product Manager” - “Zivtech, organization” Avoid: - Repeating the group name in every option if `role="group"` already exposes it reliably. - Long descriptions that make arrow navigation slow. - Visual-only icons without accessible handling. Decorative icons inside options must use `aria-hidden="true"`. WCAG 1.1.1 Non-text Content. --- ## Visual Accessibility Plan - Text contrast must meet 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text. - WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast Minimum - Input borders, active option indicators, focus indicators, and clear button affordances must meet 3:1 non-text contrast. - WCAG 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast - Active option state must not rely on color alone. Use background color plus a persistent shape, border, icon, or text treatment. - WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color - Focus styling must be visible for input and clear button using `:focus-visible`. - WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible - WCAG 2.4.13 Focus Appearance - Minimum target size is 24x24 CSS px; 44x44 is recommended for clear button and touch option rows. - WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size - At 200% zoom and 320px equivalent width, the input, popup, clear button, and options must reflow without horizontal scrolling. - WCAG 1.4.4 Resize Text - WCAG 1.4.10 Reflow - Popup height may scroll internally, but the active option must be scrolled into view when navigating with arrows. - WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured - Any opening/closing animation must respect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`. - WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions Mobile considerations: - Touching the input opens the keyboard and allows typing. - Options have enough vertical height for touch selection. - The popup must not be hidden behind the virtual keyboard where avoidable. - Clear button must have a programmatic name and sufficient target size. --- ## Content Accessibility Plan ### Labels and Instructions The combobox must have a visible label. Placeholder text is not a label. Required content: - Visible label: “Search” or a domain-specific label such as “Search people and organizations.” - Helper text: “Type to search. Use arrow keys to review suggestions.” - Status messages: concise and non-repetitive. WCAG: - 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - 2.4.6 Headings and Labels - 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions ### Option Text Option labels must be descriptive enough to distinguish similar results. For grouped people/organizations: - Person option: name plus short role or email if needed. - Organization option: organization name plus type if needed. - Avoid relying on avatars, logos, or color alone. ### Icons - Decorative search icon: `aria-hidden="true"`. - Decorative clear icon inside a named clear button: icon `aria-hidden="true"`, button has `aria-label="Clear search"`. - Icons conveying result type must either have visible text equivalents or be included in the option accessible name. WCAG: - 1.1.1 Non-text Content - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value ### Error Text If the combobox is used in a required form: - Error must be visible and specific. - Error must be associated with the input through `aria-describedby`. - Input must use `aria-invalid="true"` while invalid. Example message: “Select a result before continuing.” WCAG: - 3.3.1 Error Identification - 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions - 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value --- ## Testing Strategy ### Automated Testing Run axe-core against these states: - Initial empty closed state - Typing with popup open - Loading state - Results loaded state - Grouped results state - No results state - Option active via `aria-activedescendant` - Selected value state - Invalid required state, if applicable - Mobile/narrow viewport state Expected checks: - Valid ARIA roles and attributes - Accessible name on input and buttons - Valid `aria-controls` target - Valid `aria-activedescendant` target when present - Valid color contrast - No focusable hidden content - No duplicate ids Axe will not fully verify keyboard behavior, screen reader announcement quality, or async timing. Those require manual and Playwright keyboard tests. ### DOM Verification Tests must inspect rendered DOM, not only component state. Verify: - Input has `role="combobox"`. - Input has `aria-autocomplete="list"`. - `aria-expanded` toggles correctly. - `aria-controls` points to the listbox id. - `aria-activedescendant` is empty when no active option exists. - `aria-activedescendant` matches the active option id during arrow navigation. - Listbox has `role="listbox"`. - Options have `role="option"` and stable ids. - Active option has `aria-selected="true"`. - Loading state sets `aria-busy="true"` and then clears it. - Status region receives the expected announcement text. - Closed popup is not exposed to keyboard or screen reader users. ### Keyboard Test Cases - Tab focuses the combobox input. - Typing filters results and opens the popup. - Arrow Down opens popup and activates first option. - Arrow Down repeatedly moves through available options. - Arrow Up moves to previous option. - Disabled options, if present, are skipped. - Enter selects the active option, updates input value, closes popup, and keeps focus on input. - Escape closes popup, clears active descendant, and keeps focus on input. - Tab closes popup and moves to the next focusable element. - Clear button is reachable by Tab when visible. - Clear button responds to Enter and Space. - No keyboard trap exists. WCAG: - 2.1.1 Keyboard - 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap - 2.4.3 Focus Order - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value ### Async Loading Tests - Typing starts loading and announces “Loading suggestions.” - `aria-busy="true"` is present during fetch. - Late API responses do not overwrite newer query results. - Result count is announced after loading completes. - Empty result response announces “No suggestions found.” - Network error, if shown, is announced through the status region and visible text. WCAG: - 4.1.3 Status Messages - 3.3.1 Error Identification, if error affects user action ### Screen Reader Scenarios Test with NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver: - Focus input: announces label, combobox role, value, expanded/collapsed state, and instructions. - Type query: loading and result count are announced without interrupting typing excessively. - Arrow through options: active option is announced while focus remains on input. - Grouped options: group context is understandable. - Select option: selected value is announced or clearly reflected by input value and status. - Escape: collapsed state is communicated. - Clear: input is cleared and clear action is announced if needed. ### Visual and Responsive Tests - Focus ring visible on input and clear button. - Active option is visible and not color-only. - Component works at 200% zoom. - Component works at 320px viewport width without horizontal scrolling. - Popup does not obscure the focused input or active option. - Touch targets meet minimum size. - Reduced motion preference disables or simplifies popup animation. --- ## Implementation Tasks ### Task 1: Combobox Semantic Shell 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - Reusable combobox component file - Component styles - Unit or component test file **Structure Stub:** ```html

Type to search. Use arrow keys to review suggestions.

``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `role="combobox"`: exposes composite input role. WCAG 4.1.2. - `aria-autocomplete="list"`: exposes list autocomplete behavior. WCAG 4.1.2. - `aria-expanded`: exposes popup state. WCAG 4.1.2. - `aria-controls`: associates input with listbox. WCAG 1.3.1, 4.1.2. - `aria-activedescendant`: exposes active option while focus remains on input. WCAG 2.4.3, 4.1.2. - `aria-describedby`: associates instructions and status. WCAG 1.3.1, 3.3.2. **Keyboard Interactions:** - Tab focuses input. - Typing updates query. - Escape closes popup if open. - Tab exits normally. **Tests:** - Input has visible label. - Input has correct combobox attributes. - Helper and status ids exist and are referenced. - No invalid ARIA attributes. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - 2.1.1 Keyboard - 2.4.3 Focus Order - 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value --- ### Task 2: Popup Listbox, Options, and Grouping 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Structure Stub:** ```html
People
Jane Smith
``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `role="listbox"`: popup suggestion list. WCAG 4.1.2. - `role="option"`: selectable suggestion. WCAG 4.1.2. - Option `id`: required for `aria-activedescendant`. WCAG 4.1.2. - `aria-selected`: active/selected option state. WCAG 4.1.2. - `role="group"` and `aria-labelledby`: grouped result semantics. WCAG 1.3.1, 4.1.2. **Keyboard Interactions:** - Arrow Down activates next option. - Arrow Up activates previous option. - Group headings are skipped. - Disabled options are skipped. **Tests:** - Listbox appears only when open. - Closed popup is not exposed. - Each option has stable id. - `aria-activedescendant` references an existing option. - Group labels are visible and programmatically associated. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - 2.1.1 Keyboard - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value --- ### Task 3: Selection, Clear, and Reopen Behavior 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Structure Stub:** ```html ``` **ARIA Attributes:** - Clear button `aria-label="Clear search"` if icon-only. WCAG 4.1.2. - Decorative icon `aria-hidden="true"`. WCAG 1.1.1. - Input `aria-expanded` updates when reopening. WCAG 4.1.2. **Keyboard Interactions:** - Enter selects active option. - Click/tap selects pointer option. - Escape closes popup. - Clear button Enter/Space clears input and closes popup. - Arrow Down can reopen suggestions when results are available. **Tests:** - Enter selection updates input and closes popup. - Pointer selection updates input and returns focus to input. - Escape closes without selection. - Clear button clears value and is keyboard-operable. - Clear button target size is sufficient. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.1.1 Non-text Content - 2.1.1 Keyboard - 2.4.3 Focus Order - 2.5.8 Target Size - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value --- ### Task 4: Async Loading and Status Announcements 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Structure Stub:** ```html
Loading suggestions.
``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `aria-busy="true"` during async fetch. WCAG 4.1.3. - `role="status"` for non-urgent updates. WCAG 4.1.3. - `aria-live="polite"` for result count, loading, no results, selection. WCAG 4.1.3. - `aria-atomic="true"` so full status message is announced. WCAG 4.1.3. **Keyboard Interactions:** - Keyboard remains usable while loading. - Arrow navigation is disabled or ignored until options exist. - Focus stays on input. **Tests:** - Loading message appears and is announced. - `aria-busy` toggles true then false. - Result count announcement appears after fetch resolves. - Empty result state announces no suggestions. - Stale async response does not replace newer results. **WCAG Criteria:** - 2.1.1 Keyboard - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - 4.1.3 Status Messages --- ## a11y-Critic Review Checkpoints | Checkpoint | After Task | Focus Areas | |---|---|---| | 🔍 1 | Task 1 | Verify APG Combobox mapping, visible label, input role/state relationships, instruction association | | 🔍 2 | Task 2 | Verify listbox/option/group semantics, stable ids, `aria-activedescendant`, hidden popup behavior | | 🔍 3 | Task 3 | Verify keyboard selection, Escape behavior, clear button name, focus remains on input | | 🔍 4 | Task 4 | Verify async loading announcements, result count status, `aria-busy`, stale response handling | --- ## Architecture Overview The component consists of one editable combobox input, one controlled listbox popup, zero or more grouped option sections, one polite status region, and an optional clear button. The accessibility architecture follows the APG Combobox pattern with DOM focus retained on the input and option focus represented through `aria-activedescendant`. Primary ARIA patterns: - APG Combobox - APG Listbox popup - APG Button for clear action Primary keyboard design: - Input keeps focus. - Arrow keys navigate suggestions. - Enter selects. - Escape closes. - Tab exits. --- ## Failure Modes This plan is designed to prevent: - Moving DOM focus into the listbox instead of using `aria-activedescendant`. - Exposing a visible popup without `aria-expanded`. - Rendering options without stable ids. - Updating visual active option without updating `aria-activedescendant`. - Announcing every option update through many live regions. - Leaving hidden popup options exposed to screen readers. - Using color alone for active or selected state. - Making grouped headings keyboard stops. - Letting async result races announce or display stale suggestions. - Using `title` instead of a real accessible name. - Testing only component state instead of rendered ARIA placement. --- ## References - WAI-ARIA APG Combobox Pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/combobox/ - WAI-ARIA APG Button Pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/button/ - WCAG 2.2 Quick Reference: https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/quickref/ - Primary WCAG criteria: 1.1.1, 1.3.1, 1.3.2, 1.4.1, 1.4.3, 1.4.10, 1.4.11, 2.1.1, 2.1.2, 2.4.3, 2.4.6, 2.4.7, 2.4.11, 2.4.13, 2.5.8, 3.3.1, 3.3.2, 4.1.2, 4.1.3.
